resources.string: No contacts yet. Share your key and add theirs.
Every time you show this key a fresh one-time prekey is minted, so each contact gets a unique one.
This creates a brand-new identity and invalidates all sessions of this profile.
Could not decrypt — check the password.
The message is password-encrypted first, then hidden in the photo pixels. Send the resulting PNG as a FILE (not a compressed photo).
The message does not fit into this photo.
No hidden data found in this photo.
Signal messages are disguised as an innocent run of ordinary words.
Smart mode builds real, grammatical sentences that read like an ordinary note, at the cost of a somewhat longer message. Turn it off for the shortest possible cover text.
Permanently erases the chosen data — keys, contacts, conversations and settings. This cannot be undone.
With the lock on, biometrics or the device PIN is required to open Kryptos. The shield keeps your chats out of screenshots and the app-switcher snapshot.
Auto-decrypt: copy an encrypted message, open Kryptos, and it is shown at once. Auto-clear erases whatever Kryptos copied after the chosen time.
Pads the ciphertext to a fixed set of sizes so its length no longer hints at how long your message is. Makes the ciphertext somewhat larger.
Your messages in Chats will look like this. The encryption is identical either way — only the outer wrapping changes.
The keyboard offers a letter layout for each enabled language, and the language key switches between them. At least one language always stays on.
Soft vibration and the native click on each key press.
When the clipboard holds an encrypted message, the Kryptos keyboard decrypts it the moment it opens — copy a message in any messenger and it is revealed right there.
Encrypt anywhere. Talk over any channel.

Kryptos is a private-communication tool that works through any messenger. You write a message, Kryptos encrypts it on your device, and you send the result through WhatsApp, Telegram, SMS — anything. Only your contact\'s Kryptos can read it; the messenger only ever sees ciphertext.
Fully offline: no servers, no accounts, no analytics. The app has no network permission at all — your private keys never leave this device.
The very library the Signal app uses: post-quantum key agreement (PQXDH with Kyber) and the Double Ratchet — a fresh key for every message.
Classic asymmetric encryption with signatures, compatible with other PGP tools.
A shared passphrase: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 and AES-256-GCM.
Hides the already-encrypted message inside an ordinary photo or a run of everyday words.
Questions, ideas or a bug to report? I\'d be glad to hear from you.
Finds messages encrypted by Kryptos anywhere on screen and shows the decrypted text right over them. Only screen text is read — no keystrokes, no network.
While on-screen decryption is running, screenshots and screen recording are blocked on the whole device so decrypted text never ends up in a capture.
Finds Kryptos messages (compact code or words) shown by any app and lays the decrypted text right on top — live, as you scroll. Works only for your chat contacts, and never while Kryptos is locked.
Kryptos needs the accessibility service enabled to read the screen. Tap to open Android settings and switch it on.
Add a contact in the Kryptos app first
Nothing to encrypt — type a message first
Set a screen lock in Android settings first.
Entering this PIN on the lock screen erases all keys, chats and contacts instead of unlocking. Leave empty to disable.
This device appears to be rooted — your keys may be at risk.
A debugger is attached to Kryptos.
Running on an emulator — not a trusted environment.
A hooking framework (Frida / Xposed) is active — the app\'s memory can be read.
This APK is not signed with the original Kryptos key — it may have been modified.
Accessibility services can read your screen: %1$s. Make sure you trust each of them.
A simple symmetric mode: you and your contact agree on one shared password, then encrypt/decrypt text for any messenger. (This is not PGP — PGP has its own tab.)
Ready — paste this to your contact
Encrypted and copied to the clipboard — paste it to your contact.
Send it as a document/file. Sending it as a normal photo will recompress it and destroy the hidden data.
Saved. Send the PNG as a FILE so the hidden data survives.
Could not save the file. Try a different location.
Give this public key to anyone who wants to send you PGP messages, or to add you as a recipient (so they can verify your signature).
This is not a valid PGP public key.
Signature verified — from a saved recipient.
Decrypted, but the signature is unverified — add the sender to Recipients to verify.
Encrypt a message, send the result through any messenger, and paste the reply here to decrypt.
Encrypted & copied — paste it to your contact.
Hidden in words & copied — paste it to your contact.
Could not decrypt — wrong contact, or the message was already read once. If every NEW message from this contact fails, share your key with them again and have them re-add you.
Let your contact scan it to add you.
This is your PUBLIC key — safe to share. Your private key never leaves this device.
Erases ALL data: your keys, contacts, chats, PGP keys and settings. This cannot be undone.
Their key and session are erased from this device together with the whole conversation; your own key stays. This cannot be undone.
Securely erases this conversation. This cannot be undone.
This permanently removes this identity — its keys, contacts and messages. This cannot be undone.
Message history in all chats is wiped. Contacts stay. This cannot be undone.
All contacts\' keys, sessions and conversations are erased from this device; your own key stays. This cannot be undone.
The private key is destroyed permanently — messages encrypted to it can never be decrypted again.
Type your message inside the keyboard and encrypt it there — only the encrypted result reaches the messenger, so it can never store the plaintext. Applies the next time the keyboard opens.

Context-aware completions, next-word predictions and typo fixes from built-in offline dictionaries (Russian and English). Auto-correction fixes a mistyped word the moment you finish it — keyboard-slip typos and a missing space (какдела → как дела). Backspace right after — or a tap on your word shown «quoted» in the strip — undoes the fix and the keyboard never touches that word again. Everything is learned right on the device, stored encrypted, and disabled in password fields. Changes apply the next time the keyboard opens.
The personal dictionary learned from your typing is erased. The built-in dictionaries stay.
No recent emoji yet — pick a category below
